---
title: "Identity Modernization Validation Suite"
subtitle: "Conformance tests and validation criteria for the identity domain"
doc-type: domain-validation-suite
audience: [Customer, Assessor, Adapter Owner]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
domain: identity
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/modernization-specs/identity/identity.qmd"
  - "src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# Identity Modernization Validation Suite

## Scope

Conformance tests and validation criteria for the identity domain
under the UIAO substrate. Pairs with the
[Identity Modernization Spec](../../../modernization-specs/identity/identity.qmd).
The suite defines what an identity modernization program must satisfy
to demonstrate substrate conformance, distinct from any single-adapter
test. Adapter-level tests live under
[Adapter Validation Suites](../../adapters/index.html); this suite is
the cross-adapter conformance gate.

![Identity validation suite diagram](images/identity-image-01-identity-validation-suite-diagra.png){#fig-identity-image-01 fig-alt="Center shows a \"Conformance Gate\" rectangle. Inputs flow in from the left as four test categories: Schema Conformance, Provenance Chain Integrity, Drift Detection Coverage, Boundary Enforcement. Each test category lists 3-5 specific checks underneath. Outputs flow out the right as a Pass/Fail signal plus the canonical claim emission \"domain-conformance-evidence\". Below the gate, a footer band shows the four canon invariants that must hold. Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic." width="85%"}

## Test categories

### 1. Schema conformance

Every identity adapter must:

- Declare `mission-class: identity` and `object-identity-only: true`.
- Emit claims that validate against the identity claim schema.
- Carry the canonical attribute set (`assignment_scope`,
  `organizational_role`, `classification_clearance`) where
  applicable.
- Pass `schema-validation.yml` and `metadata-validator.yml` on every
  PR.

### 2. Provenance chain integrity

For each emitted identity claim:

- `issuer_identity` resolves to a verified identity-plane object.
- `source_classification` is one of `authoritative` / `derived` /
  `synthesized`.
- `transformation_chain` is non-empty and append-only.
- `lineage_hash` is present and matches recomputation.
- `signature` is present when the adapter is `certificate-anchored`.

### 3. Drift detection coverage

The domain must demonstrate detection for:

- `DRIFT-IDENTITY` (issuer cannot resolve) — runtime adapter or CI.
- `DRIFT-SCHEMA` (attribute structure mismatch) — CI substrate walker.
- `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` (chain breakage at AD ↔ Entra cutover) — CI +
  runtime.
- `DRIFT-AUTHZ` (claim outside consent envelope) — runtime adapter.

P1 findings (active-use claims with provenance/identity drift) must
halt and alert immediately.

### 4. Boundary enforcement

- All identity adapters declare `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate` (or a
  named exception per ADR-059).
- Microsoft Graph adapters resolve their endpoint via
  `_graph_clouds.resolve_graph_base()` rather than hardcoding.
- Unknown clouds fail closed at construction.

## Evidence expectations

A conformant identity domain produces, per cycle:

- **Per-claim provenance records** — full envelope on every emission.
- **Drift findings** — emitted into the canonical evidence chain.
- **AODIM attribute snapshots** — canonical attribute set for every
  identity in scope, derivable to the policy engine.
- **OSCAL identity-related artifacts** — SSP IA/AC narratives, KSI
  identity-pillar dashboard entries, Component Definitions for
  identity adapters.

## Conformance gates

Three gates define the conformance level:

| Gate | What it requires | Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **T1 — schema** | Adapters validate against canon schemas | Required |
| **T2 — provenance** | Provenance envelope complete on every claim | Required |
| **T3 — drift coverage** | Domain-level drift detection across all four classes | Target |

T1 and T2 are minimum bars; T3 is the maturity bar that distinguishes
target-state conformance from baseline.

## Drift procedures

When a drift finding fires:

1. Severity computed deterministically per
   [`16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd`](../../../../docs/16_DriftDetectionStandard.qmd).
2. Auto-remediation runs for deterministic fixes.
3. Escalation path lands at `Canon Steward | Architecture Lead | CISO`
   per the standard.
4. Remediation contract emitted into the evidence chain.

## Honest limits

- T3 (drift coverage) is partial today across the adapter fleet.
  Programs running with T1 + T2 are at substrate-baseline conformance
  but not yet at target-state.
- The AODIM attribute set is opinionated; programs that deviate from
  it cannot demonstrate full T3 conformance even if their substrate
  technically detects drift.
- Cross-adapter correlation (e.g. confirming the same identity
  resolves identically across PIV, Entra, and HR adapters) is a
  domain-level test that requires multiple adapters running
  concurrently.

## Related documents

- [Modernization Spec — Identity](../../../modernization-specs/identity/identity.qmd)
- [ADR-008: Zero Trust Identity](../../../../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md)
- [Validation Suites Index](../../../index.qmd)
